title: S33R More Food
description: This vanilla style mod adds over 50 new recipes to cook based on the existing food items, like Sweet Berry Tea and Honey Steak Sandwiches.
body:
This mod adds over 50 new food items to the game, with recipes based on existing Minecraft items. It adds various drinks such as Azalea Tea and Golden Carrot Juice, meals like Fish Skewers and Chicken Salad, and sweets like Lilac Cookies and Chorus Fruit Candy. Many of the food items inflict effects and all give health/saturation based on their ingredients. A number of the added items are also placeable, so you can add more decorative detail to your table and kitchen builds. Speaking of more detail, this mod was released alongside a new resource pack I made, just search S33R Silverware on the Planet Minecraft website to see my CIT resource pack. It adds silverware and cups which line up nicely with the food models added by this mod, and is shown off in the Images section of this page.

All of the recipes added by the S33R More Food mod are listed in the Spoilers tab below. Recipes with the crossed arrows above the output can be placed in any pattern in the crafting table, and outputs surrounded with a pink outline can be placed on a block. You can obtain raw cactus slices by right clicking a cactus with a sword, and you can right click cauldrons with empty jars to scoop up water. Toppings can be crafted with either plain pancakes or toast to flavor it, and pies are eaten in two bites to make a pie slice. Placed pies can also be eaten by right clicking, similar to a cake.

I also created a companion resource pack for this mod, the [Silverware pack](https://modrinth.com).

Published alongside both this mod and the Silverware pack is my new Discord server!! :OO If you have suggestions or comments about the creations I've published, want to get sneak peaks on the projects I'm working on, or want to join a community of fellow artists, coders, and Minecraft enjoyers, consider joining the Empyr3 \o/

I'd really love to start building a community where people can show off the Minecraft projects they're working on as well as get information on how to start making their own. I feel one of the biggest barriers for making mods and resource packs is just learning how to start, I've never seen a class on Minecraft coding before lmao. So if I can start creating a place where people can find info they need to start making, I'll have achieved my life's goals kek.


Feel free to visit o/ https://discord.gg/zrxVyCZa5G

~ Seer
